garage.title: Garage Analytics
garage.subtitle: Comprehensive cost analysis across all vehicles
garage.exportPdfFailed: Failed to export PDF. Please try again.

empty.title: No Vehicles Yet
empty.body: Add vehicles to your garage to start tracking garage-wide analytics

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: The Garage Analytics page provides a comprehensive view of all vehicles in your garage, allowing you to analyze total costs, compare vehicle expenses, identify spending patterns, and make informed decisions about your garage management strategy.

costSummary.title: Garage Cost Summary
costSummary.garageValueDesc: Total purchase price of all vehicles.
costSummary.maintenanceDesc: Total service and repair costs across all vehicles.
costSummary.fuelDesc: Total fuel expenses for the entire garage.
costSummary.insuranceDesc: Combined insurance costs for all vehicles.

categoryBreakdown.title: Cost Breakdown by Category
categoryBreakdown.body: The pie chart shows how garage spending is distributed across different categories. Use this to identify which expense types consume the largest portion of your budget. Each category displays its percentage of total garage costs.

costByVehicle.title: Cost by Vehicle
costByVehicle.intro: The bar chart and detailed table compare costs across all vehicles. This helps you:
costByVehicle.item1: Identify which vehicles are most expensive to maintain
costByVehicle.item2: Compare fuel efficiency across your garage
costByVehicle.item3: Make data-driven decisions about vehicle replacement
costByVehicle.item4: Spot vehicles with unusually high maintenance costs
costByVehicle.item5: Budget for individual vehicle operating costs

monthlyTrend.title: Monthly Spending Trend
monthlyTrend.intro: The stacked bar chart shows garage-wide spending over time, separated into maintenance and fuel costs. Trend lines overlay the bars to show rolling averages:
monthlyTrend.item1: Identify seasonal spending patterns across your garage
monthlyTrend.item2: Spot months with unusually high or low expenses
monthlyTrend.item3: Track whether garage costs are trending up or down over time

comparisonTable.title: Vehicle Cost Comparison Table
comparisonTable.intro: The detailed table at the bottom provides a side-by-side comparison of all vehicles:
comparisonTable.sortIntro: Sort this data mentally to identify:
comparisonTable.item4: Candidates for replacement based on total cost of ownership

exportOptions.title: Export Options
exportOptions.csvDesc: Download garage data in spreadsheet format for further analysis, budgeting, or reporting.
exportOptions.pdfDesc: Generate a professional garage report with summary tables and totals for stakeholders or records.

insights.title: Garage Management Insights
insights.intro: Use Garage Analytics to answer key questions:
insights.budgetDesc: What should I budget for garage expenses next quarter/year?
insights.replacementDesc: Which vehicles should I consider replacing due to high operating costs?
insights.allocationDesc: How much does each vehicle contribute to total garage costs?
insights.efficiencyDesc: Which vehicles have the best fuel economy or lowest maintenance costs?
insights.trendDesc: Are garage costs increasing, decreasing, or stable over time?
insights.categoryDesc: Where should I focus cost reduction efforts (fuel, maintenance, insurance)?

tips.title: Tips for Effective Garage Management
tips.item1: Regularly review the monthly trend to catch cost increases early
tips.item2: Compare similar vehicles to identify maintenance outliers
tips.item3: Track fuel costs to identify opportunities for fuel economy improvements
tips.item4: Use exports for quarterly or annual garage reporting
tips.item5: Monitor the cost breakdown chart to ensure balanced spending across categories
tips.item6: Consider total cost of ownership (not just purchase price) when evaluating vehicles
tips.item7: Look for seasonal patterns that can help with budget planning
tips.item8: Use individual vehicle analytics for deeper insights on specific vehicles

dataRequirements.title: Data Requirements
dataRequirements.item1: Add all vehicles to your garage with purchase prices
dataRequirements.item2: Enter service records for all vehicles
dataRequirements.item3: Track fuel records for fuel cost analysis
dataRequirements.item4: Include insurance and tax expenses in service records
dataRequirements.item5: Maintain at least 3-6 months of data for meaningful trends
dataRequirements.item6: Keep records consistent across all vehicles for fair comparisons

vehicle.title: Analytics & Reports
vehicle.fillAllDates: Please fill in all date fields
vehicle.trendIncreasingDesc: Your costs are trending upward over time
vehicle.trendDecreasingDesc: Your costs are trending downward over time
vehicle.trendStableDesc: Your spending pattern is relatively consistent

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: The Analytics page provides comprehensive insights into your vehicle's costs, fuel efficiency, maintenance patterns, and spending trends. Use these insights to make informed decisions about your vehicle maintenance and budget.

costAnalysis.title: Cost Analysis
costAnalysis.totalCostDesc: Sum of all service and fuel expenses tracked.
costAnalysis.avgMonthlyDesc: Total cost divided by months of tracking.
costAnalysis.costPerMileDesc: Total cost divided by total miles driven (if mileage data available).
costAnalysis.serviceBreakdownDesc: Shows which types of services cost the most.

rollingAverages.title: Rolling Averages
rollingAverages.threeMonthDesc: Average monthly cost over the last 3 months.
rollingAverages.sixMonthDesc: Average monthly cost over the last 6 months.
rollingAverages.twelveMonthDesc: Average monthly cost over the last 12 months.
rollingAverages.note: Rolling averages smooth out temporary spikes and help identify long-term trends. Compare these values to spot whether your costs are trending up or down.

trendDirection.title: Trend Direction
trendDirection.increasingDesc: Recent costs are trending upward. May indicate aging vehicle or deferred maintenance catching up.
trendDirection.decreasingDesc: Recent costs are trending downward. Could mean major repairs are complete or reduced usage.
trendDirection.stableDesc: Costs are consistent month-to-month with minimal variation.

anomalies.title: Spending Anomalies
anomalies.body: Anomaly detection automatically identifies months where spending was significantly higher or lower than your average. This uses statistical analysis to find outliers.
anomalies.note: Anomalies aren't necessarily bad - they often represent one-time events like major repairs, annual registrations, or deferred maintenance. Review these to understand unusual spending patterns.

fuelEconomy.title: Fuel Economy
fuelEconomy.mpgCalcDesc: Automatically calculated from consecutive full-tank fill-ups. Partial fills are not used in MPG calculations.
fuelEconomy.averageMpgDesc: Mean fuel economy across all full-tank fill-ups.
fuelEconomy.recentMpgDesc: Average of the last 5 full-tank fill-ups.
fuelEconomy.note: Declining fuel economy may indicate maintenance needs (air filter, spark plugs, tire pressure) or driving habit changes. Significant drops warrant investigation.

vendorSpending.title: Vendor Spending
vendorSpending.body: Shows which service providers you've spent the most with. Use this to identify your primary vendors and compare costs between different shops for similar services.

seasonal.title: Seasonal Patterns
seasonal.body: Spending grouped by season to identify patterns. Many vehicles have seasonal maintenance needs:
seasonal.summerDesc: A/C service, road trips, cooling system

periodComparison.title: Period Comparison
periodComparison.body: Compare costs between two custom date ranges to see how spending has changed year-over-year or between different time periods. Useful for identifying trends and validating budget changes.

predictions.title: Maintenance Predictions
predictions.body: Based on your service history, the system predicts when recurring maintenance is due. Predictions are more accurate with more historical data.

export.title: Export Options
export.csvDesc: Download all analytics data in spreadsheet format for further analysis.
export.pdfDesc: Generate a professional report with charts and summaries for records or tax purposes.

tips.title: Tips for Best Results
tips.enterAllRecords: Enter all service and fuel records for complete analytics
tips.includeMileage: Include mileage data for cost-per-mile calculations
tips.markFullTank: Mark fuel fill-ups as "full tank" for accurate MPG tracking
tips.addVendorNames: Add vendor names to identify spending patterns
tips.trackMonths: Track at least 3-6 months of data for meaningful trends
tips.reviewAnomalies: Review anomalies to understand unusual expenses
